I don’t know what maybe viewers saw in recent episodes that would make you think differently, but I think Bryce is a very emotionally mature person. And I think him and Trinity, through what I saw in my time there, were able to have great communication skills. And when you’re looking for communication in a relationship, one of the most important things is how you communicate when you’re upset and things aren’t going well. And I feel that both of them, in my time there seeing them, had a really good ability to be like, “Hey, I’m upset, but I can articulate it very well.” And then they were able to find common ground.

title: All the Stars Who Came Out for the Knicks Parade in New York City
summary: New York City celebrates the Knicks in a historic parade. See all the celebs who showed up including Timothee Chalamet, Ben Stiller, Martha Stewart and more.
story: The New York Knicks overcame a 53-year drought on June 13 when they beat the San Antonio Spurs and took the NBA title, finally bringing home the Larry O’Brien Championship Trophy.

Cheering on their beloved team, New Yorkers poured into the streets of the city to commemorate the magical moment — and the celebration hasn’t stopped. Ecstatic fans get a chance to give the Knicks their flowers on June 18 in a ticker-tape parade that’s one for the ages.

Among the millions of fans lining the streets of lower Manhattan, are some of the Knicks’ biggest celeb supporters including Ben Stiller, Timothée Chalamet, Chris Rock, Spike Lee, Martha Stewart and many others. Read on to see who’s on location to celebrate the Knicks in this history-making parade.
Ben Stiller

One of the Knicks’ biggest die-hard fans is actor and director Ben Stiller. Born and raised in New York City, Stiller has been courtside for countless games and is currently working on a docuseries on his favorite NBA team in tandem with HBO and A24. Of the Knicks championship win, Stiller posted on X saying, “It’s a beautiful day in New York and Knicks are NBC Champions and I’m watching our guys do all the shows and life is good today.”
Timothée Chalamet

Often spotted front and center at Madison Square Garden, Oscar nominee Timothée Chalamet has been a Knicks fan through thick and thin. The “Dune” actor was on hand in San Antonio when the Knicks took the championship and notably shouted in front of ESPN cameras, “Way rather this than the Oscars! Come on, baby!”
Chris Rock

A diehard fan, comedian Chris Rock joined along the parade route saying, “This is the most amazing day of my life,” on TODAY’s Threads account. “It’s this, then the birth of my children,” he joked. The “Madagascar” star is a staple at Knicks games and was spotted with Adam Sandler at Game 4 of the championships.
Spike Lee

Undeniably the biggest Knicks fan of them all, Spike Lee was naturally front and center for the championship parade to cheer on his winning team. A season ticket holder since 1985, Lee told MSG Networks during an interview that he’d never been to a parade before saying, “I’m glad it’s this one!”
Martha Stewart

A showstopper in her own right, Martha Stewart has been a Knicks fan for decades. The 84-year-old even had her toe broken while sitting courtside in a 2025 game between the Knicks and the Indiana Pacers. On an episode of “The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon,” the lifestyle mogel revealed that she unwisely wore open-toed shoes to the game and suffered a Bruson fracture after Knicks guard Jalen Brunson accidentally came “crashing down” on her toe.
Mariska Hargitay

To know Mariska Hargitay is to know that the “Law & Order: SVU” star is the Knicks No. 1 fan. A true face-painter at heart, the actor joked to The Hollywood Reporter that the Knicks comeback in Game 4 of the NBA Finals upstaged even her wedding night: “I love my husband, and our wedding night was great and all, but I think it might have been the greatest night of my life.” Hargitay also called the win one of “the best moments in sports,” telling THR in a statement, “OH MY GOD, I LOVE THIS TEAM!!!”
Tracy Morgan

One of the Knicks most emotional fans is “30 Rock” alum Tracy Morgan. The comedian has notably been spotted in tears at more than one game. Upon winning the championship against the Spurs, Morgan could be seen crying on the court. In an interview with NBA TV, Morgan excitedly said, “Boom!’ That’s all it is. You don’t feel nothing. It’s instant. It’s ‘Boom!’”
Fat Joe

Fat Joe, one of hip hop’s most legendary artists, is one of the Knicks biggest superfans. During a June 9 interview with Variety, the rapper said, “It meant everything to me to watch the Knicks win the championship. I’ve been going to games since I was a kid sitting in the nosebleeds, so it still feels like a dream to have been part of this ride.”

title: Carley Fortune Details the ‘Long’ Call She Had with Meghan Markle About ‘Meet Me at the Lake’ Adaptation
summary: Carley Fortune tells Jenna about where the adaptation for Meet Me at the Lake stands and her relationship with Meghan Markle.
story: Carley Fortune’s romance novels are hitting the screen. “Every Year After,” the Amazon Prime adaptation of her debut “Every Summer After,” premiered in June, and there are more to come.
During the June 18 episode of “Open Book with Jenna,” Fortune gave some details about where the adaptation for “Meet Me at the Lake,” her second novel, stands.

Back in August 2023, news broke that Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, had purchased the rights to the book for an eventual Netflix adaptation through their production company, Archewell Productions. Fortune confirmed the news to The Independent.
“It is so wild,” Fortune, 42, admitted to Jenna about working with the Duke and Duchess of Sussex.
Granting Archewell the rights to the novel wasn’t based on buzz surrounding the royal couple. Rather, it was about a shared understanding of the story’s core, the author said.
Fortune said she signed over the rights to Archewell  after a “really good, long call” with Markle and Archewell film producer Tracy Ryerson.

“I felt like they really understood the story and what mattered about the story,” Fortune said. “It’s a romance, but it’s very much about a mother and daughter, too, and we talked a lot about parenthood, and also about how these love stories are about so much more. It’s a story about grief, it’s a story about mental health, and so I felt like it was in good hands with them.”
More from Carley Fortune
Carley Fortune Wants Her Teenage Self To Feel Seen By ‘Every Year After’How ‘Every Year After’ Handles the Romance Genre’s Most Controversial Plot Twist
Carley Fortune Wants Her Teenage Self To Feel Seen By ‘Every Year After’
How ‘Every Year After’ Handles the Romance Genre’s Most Controversial Plot Twist
She said the project is “still in development.” A director is attached but the script is “still being revised.”
Fortune said her relationship with the royal couple remains exclusively professional. “No, I am not hanging out with Harry and Meghan on weekends,” Fortune joked.
“You can, you could!” Jenna replied.

“Meet Me at the Lake” follows strangers Fern Brookbanks and Will Baxter as they are sent on “a daylong adventure where they make a promise one keeps and the other breaks, with life-changing effects,” per Fortune’s website.

Among the mental health topics Fortune is entrusting the production team to convey is postpartum obsessive-compulsive disorder, or postpartum OCD, which the author herself experienced, she told Jenna.

“What I have found through books is I can weave in things that are really important to me and people engage with them because they are reading this romance, they know it’s going to have a happy ending,” Fortune said. “But they are learning about mental health.”
Of postpartum OCD, she added that the symptoms are “very very hard to talk about” — but she tackles it in the novel anyway.

“A lot of people don’t know about it, I didn’t know about it, and I’ve found it’s been so wonderful to have heard from readers who didn’t know what they were going through until they read the book, and who found courage to talk to a loved one or a doctor,” she said. “I think there’s just such power in that … It’s been a way that I can connect with people.”

title: Olivia Wilde on the Moment She Realized Her Relationship with Jason Sudeikis Was Over
summary: Olivia Wilde is opening up about the conversation that led to her split from ex-fiancé Jason Sudeikis.
story: Olivia Wilde is opening up about the conversation that led to her split from ex-fiancé Jason Sudeikis.

The 42-year-old actor and director was a guest on the June 17 episode of the “Call Her Daddy” podcast and opened up about her past relationships, including with the “Ted Lasso” star, 50. Wilde and Sudeikis, who share 12-year-old son Otis and 9-year-old daughter Daisy, started dating in 2011 and got engaged in 2013. The two called it quits in November 2020.

During her chat with host Alex Cooper, Wilde said that her upcoming movie “The Invite,” which she directs and also stars in, focuses on a couple coming to the realization that they’ve grown apart — a challenging moment she experienced with Sudeikis.

“There was never any public understanding, of course, because they’re not a part of our private lives, but of that breakup,” she said about calling off their engagement. “It’s no surprise to me that I ended up making a movie about relationships and the complexity of determining whether a relationship is over, because it is not an overnight process. It’s very difficult.”
She added that her film, which hits theaters June 26, is centered on “the idea that relationships can come to a place where you become strangers.”

Wilde then recalled the exact date and moment she realized it was time to end her relationship with Sudeikis. The “Don’t Worry Darling” director remembered it being her birthday on March 10, 2020, and attending a party with her then-fiancé.
She shared that the two “had been having a rough time there for a while.”
“We had a real bumpy, bumpy ride,” she added before going into detail about the pivotal conversation.
During the drive home from the birthday party her friends threw, Wilde asked Sudeikis if he bought her a present.
“He said, ‘What would I get you Olivia? I don’t know you,’” she told Cooper. “And he wasn’t wrong. We didn’t know each other anymore.”

Wilde said the interaction was one of the inspirations behind her movie because she wanted to explore the idea of two people getting to a place in their relationship where they stopped being curious about each other.
“That was when we realized it was over, and it was f------ tough. It brought us to the place of, like, ‘OK, this is done. We’re going to end this,’” she continued.
Wilde said that two days later the exes had to go into lockdown with each other when the COVID-19 pandemic began.
Sudeikis previously discussed their split in a 2021 interview with GQ where he said he wasn’t sure why they broke up.

“I’ll have a better understanding of why in a year,” he said at the time. “And an even better one in two, and an even greater one in five, and it’ll go from being, you know, a book of my life to becoming a chapter to a paragraph to a line to a word to a doodle.”
He also said he needed to process the experience and learn from it.
“You take some responsibility for it, hold yourself accountable for what you do, but then also endeavor to learn something beyond the obvious from it,” Sudeikis shared.

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Star Beatriz Reveals How She Feels Watching Gabriel Move On With Jen
summary: “Love Island USA” Season 8 star Beatriz Hatz’s reveals to TODAY.com if there is any “beef” with Melanie and how she feels about Gabriel moving on with Jen.
story: “Love Island USA” Season 8 star Beatriz Hatz’s time in the villa was short but meaningful.

The 25-year-old Paralympian from San Diego was an OG islander this season who entertained fans with her decision on the first episode to explore a connection with Gabriel Vasconcelos over Sean Reifel, her blunt conversations with Melanie Moreno, and her jokes about her limb difference.

But in Episode 9, Beatriz’s time in the villa ended abruptly after America voted to partner Gabriel with new bombshell Jen Terry, leaving the athlete single and vulnerable. She was dumped from the island after Corbin Mims chose to couple up with Melanie and shared a tearful goodbye with Gabriel.
Since then, Beatriz tells TODAY.com that she has been following all the drama that has unfolded with the islanders after her departure, and what fans have been saying about the cast on social media.

Below, Beatriz opens up about her experience on the show, revealing if there is any “beef” between her and Melanie after their argument and how she felt seeing Gabriel move on so quickly. She also shares her thoughts on being a representation of people with disabilities, how she thinks the islanders will behave when Casa Amor starts next week and which islander is misunderstood.
This interview has been edited and condensed for clarity.

It was so cool when I got cast. I was like, “Oh my gosh, no way! This is insane.” They reassured me that, like, “Hey, we picked you because we like you, not because you’re disabled.” But these two things are amazing: I get to be on the show, and I also get to be the representation. There’s a lot of stigmas around disability that I’m just happy that I was there to break these stereotypes.

I think I was able to talk a bit more with the girls about that, and they were able to ask me questions. It was really cool to share this knowledge. Then with the boys, they were so interested in the running blade. It was like a car. They were like, “Oh, what is this? What does it feel like? How heavy is it?” I’m sitting there, show and tell, like, “Hey, do you want to hold it? Do you want to hold the prosthetic? You can.” I felt so much love from my fellow cast members and it was really cool that everybody was so supportive about it.

I’ve been following the show. I’ve been watching the current episodes. I need to go back and watch from the beginning because I want to see how I’m portrayed, what was going on, all the things that were happening that I didn’t particularly know about. I’m curious.

I think I’ve been seeing everybody call Corbin AI. OK, no, he’s a person. He’s multifaceted. He’s got different layers, and maybe we’re just not seeing all of them. I think that’s a misconception. I think everybody has different sides of themselves. Whether or not you’re willing to show that to everybody or just maybe a select few of people, that’s a different thing.

I think two things can be true at once. I think he may still be playing the game. But also I do think that he’s an individual who will probably have different layers, and maybe I just didn’t get to know those sides of him.

We’ve actually told all the girls and all the people in there, like, “Hey, no matter what, when we get out of here, we’re the ones that know what actually happened in here. I know that there’s going to be things that we can’t talk about, but we know what happened. So that’s the only thing that matters.” There’s no one-sided beef, there’s no beef at all. I think when you have friends around you, your friends are able to tell you, “Hey, I actually don’t like this.’ Or, ‘I do like this.’ Or, ‘Can you help me with this? Can you help me with that?” I think that’s just different levels of friendships that maybe people aren’t used to seeing, or maybe they’re not used to seeing that communication style. Like I said, we made up. I shared something very deep. She did as well. You continuously develop those friendships. And what makes a friendship is if you can have a discussion and be open and (say), “OK, I’m willing to take responsibility for this and this.” And then, OK, squash (it). We are now growing our connection as friends. That deepens the friendship.

I think we all have our people that we gravitate the most towards and hang out the most with. I think Melanie and I were really able to relate when we speak Spanish, what our cultural similarities are. That was something that was very deep. But then I have my friendship with Trinity (Tatum) and Kayda (Reese Bosse). We just will shoot the s--- all day and make jokes. Kenzie (Annis), she’s really bubbly. Aniya (Harvey), she’s very wise. We each had different little types of friendships. But I would say that I’m the closest with Trinity and Kayda.

When I left the villa, I felt that the connection was strong. Seeing how he was emotional over me leaving, as well, I think strengthened that for me. I had said, “Hey, he knows where to find me.” I think that’s still true, but also seeing how he is moving in there — and yes, it’s “Love Island.” So I don’t know if he would even come out of this single or what. But I know whatever is meant to happen is meant to happen. If he ends up with Jen, then I hope they continue to strengthen their connection, in and outside the villa. If not, then I hope he finds the person that he needs to make himself happy.

Oh, my girls had my back! Oh my gosh. I figured they would say something and when they did, I was like, “Oh yeah, they’re real ones, for sure.” I was surprised. But, at the same time, it’s like, what can you expect? It is “Love Island.” You are expected to recouple. But I was like, “Dang, same night that I left, you’re already … you know.” It is what it is, it’s not like I’m mad about it. It’s not anything like that.

I thought that Sol (Dean) and Sincere’s (Rhea) connection was deeper. I wasn’t sure, but I think that his connection with Melanie is very deep. So I really didn’t know what to expect with that. I was on the edge of my seat (watching). I was like, “I don’t know what either of them are going to do right now.”

I think what typically happens is the men explore more or that they experience some short-term memory loss a lot quicker than the girls do. They’re a lot more emotionally available, quicker than the girls, but I can’t really predict too much. I think Corbin will be willing to explore. I think Sincere will also be willing to explore. I think Gabriel, for sure, is exploring. Those are the top three. I think all the boys will explore. I think Zach (Georgiou), KC (Chandler) and Bryce (Dettloff), they will probably still explore, but keep their current couple in mind.
Then out of the girls, I could see Kenzie exploring. It’ll be really interesting.

My highlight from being in the villa was becoming friends with Trinity and Kayda. I wouldn’t change anything. I think my experience was what it was and it was meant to be short-lived. It might be a blessing in disguise, who knows.

title: What Time Is the Knicks Parade? How to Watch the Ticker-Tape Parade on TV
summary: The New York Knicks are ready to celebrate the team’s first championship in 53 years. Find out how to tune into the parade, what time it starts and how to watch it on TV.
story: The New York Knicks are ready to paint the streets of New York City orange and blue to celebrate the team’s first championship in 53 years.

On June 13, the Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs in game five of the best of seven series. The winning team was led by coach Mike Brown and star players Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, Josh Hart, Mikal Bridges and OG Anunoby. Now, the team is ready to go all out after ending their title drought with the franchise’s first ticker-tape parade.
Follow along with our live blog.
Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced the celebration is happening on Thursday, June 18, and that there will be a City Hall ceremony to honor the team with Keys to the City.

“For more than 50 years, New Yorkers have waited for this moment. Through near misses, heartbreak and a hope that every year could be our year, this city never stopped believing in the Knicks. And this team fulfilled that hope with grit, resilience and heart — just like the five boroughs itself,” the mayor said in a June 13 statement.

“New Yorkers have cheered for our team from packed living rooms in the Bronx to watch parties in Brooklyn, from bars in Queens to Staten Island to Manhattan, and Madison Square Garden itself. Now it’s time for our city to celebrate together. Bing bong.”
He also shared that the city will be lean into orange and blue as City Hall and other municipal buildings will be illuminated with the colors as the team makes its way through Manhattan.
Find out how to watch and join in on the fun from home, below.
How to Follow the Knicks Championship Parade Route
The Knicks championship parade will be held on Thursday, June 18, and kick off at 10 a.m. ET.

The parade will unfold in lower Manhattan. It will begin in Battery Park. It will then make its way north on Broadway, passing by the Canyon of Heroes. The festivities will end at City Hall, where a ceremony will take place and Keys to the City will be presented.
How to Watch the Knicks Championship Parade on TV
Locals who want to avoid the traffic but still celebrate the home team can watch the parade on MSG Network, ABC7 New York and NBC 4 New York.
For those tuning in out of state, the parade will air on NBA TV. It will stream on the NBA app, as well.

title: Jordyn Woods Reacts to a ‘Love Island USA’ Contestant Wearing Her Dress: ‘Most Excited I’ve Been. Well —’
summary: Jordyn Woods talks about her lucky handbag, Knicks superstitions and her dress being on Love Island USA.
story: After the Knicks won the NBA Finals, the franchise’s first championship in 53 years, center Karl-Anthony Towns gave a shoutout to fiancée Jordyn Woods’ handbag.

“You always pray you’re going to pick the right woman for you and I know I did in my fiancée. I damn sure know now with that bag. That bag held it down. Shout out to Woods by Jordyn,” he said in a postgame interview June 13.

While Towns and the New York Knicks reveling in the post-championship glow, complete with a tequila toast on the TODAY plaza, Woods is enjoying her own moment in the spotlight, with her fashion designs at the center of major pop culture moments.
Woods’ signature orange ostrich faux leather clutch — sold out and now available for pre-order on the Woods by Jordyn brand’s website — became the Knicks’ de-facto “lucky charm” during the playoffs.
“I made this sample for the playoffs from my brand, Woods by Jordyn, and I started wearing it,” Woods said on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle June 17. “We won a game, it became a superstition for me.”
But it wasn’t the only one, she tells TODAY.com.

“My watch, I wouldn’t take off. My necklace, I wouldn’t take off. Outfit wise, I wasn’t too superstitious, but as long as the bag was with me, we were good,” she says. “I think a lot more people have superstitions than we think. A lot of people I met on the street told me they didn’t wash their shirt for 10 games, which I wouldn’t do, but they do.”

Woods says the most stressful moment of the series — which included a 29-point comeback win in Game 4 and Towns fouling out with less than two minutes on the clock in Game 5 — came when she didn’t have that bag.

Pinpointing the most heart-racing, nerve-wracking moment of the NBA Finals, Woods says: “Probably when I couldn’t bring my bag in, and everyone was blaming me.” (During Game 3, the first at home in New York’s Madison Square Garden, President Trump’s attendance meant a “strict no-bag policy” and “TSA-style screening procedures.” The Knicks lost.)
When that bag returned for Game 4 at New York, it coincided with a miraculous comeback that set the stage for victory in Game 5 in San Antonio.
But that wasn’t the only big moment for her brand this week.
On June 16 episode of “Love Island USA,” contestant Aniya Harvey wore an orange and purple mesh bodycon dress from Woods by Jordyn.

title: Sofia Vergara Says 1 of Her Favorite World Cup Memories Is Watching the ‘Spectacular’ 1994 Colombian Team
summary: Sofia Vergara shares her earliest and most favorite memories of watching the World Cup, and her involvement in the 2026 tournament.
story: Sofia Vergara grew up watching the FIFA World Cup.
The actor and entrepreneur doesn’t remember a time when it wasn’t part of her life growing up in Colombia, which makes this year’s soccer tournament even more special.

Vergara is not only a World Cup ambassador for Telemundo, she and Octavia Spencer also have a Detect the SOS commercial airing during the matches. Inspired by her mother’s experience with high blood pressure, the “Modern Family” star went on a mission to spread awareness about uACR tests that detect early kidney damage.
“We’ve had a great time, we made a really cute commercial,” Vergara tells TODAY.com in Spanish. “The important thing is that we have this platform now with the World Cup.”
Below, Vergara shares her World Cup memories, as well as what she’s most looking forward to as the tournament is underway.
Well, I’m Colombian. We grew up with that. It’s kind of impossible to pinpoint my first memory, but some of my best memories are from when I was a little older, around ‘94.

We had an amazing team with players like (René) Higuita, (Carlos) “El Pibe” Valderrama. We had a spectacular group, it was the Dream Team then in ‘94. And we went to the World Cup, and I’ll always remember that World Cup as something very, very special.

There are so many little sacrifices you have to make along the way, always. For example, when my son was very young, many times I couldn’t be there for school things, for his hobbies, or I probably missed a birthday or two because I was traveling for work.
All those things are very difficult. I think the most (difficult) thing is when you miss things with your children.
(However), my whole family has always supported me, all my friends from Colombia, we’re all very close.
I think having such a close group of people who love and support you always gives you strength and makes you want to do fun and good things.
Everything in general! I’m lucky that I’m working on this mission and with Telemundo.
I’m Telemundo’s ambassador for the World Cup, so I’m going to be able to go to a lot of games. I’m going to be able to be at the final with a really great spot. So that has me super excited!

title: Jordyn Woods, Who Lost Her Dad When She Was 19, Gives Hopeful Message to Sheinelle and Her Children
summary: Jordyn Woods, whose father died when she was a teenager, has some words of support for Sheinelle Jones and her kids after the death of Uche Ojeh.
story: Jordyn Woods, whose father died when she was a teenager, has some words of support for Sheinelle Jones and her kids as they navigate life after the death of Sheinelle’s husband and father of her three children, Uche Ojeh.

“I just want to say as someone who lost their dad at such a young age, and to your children or anyone out there — it doesn’t get easier, you just get stronger and you learn to deal with that and honor them through every step of the way,” Woods, 28, said during a June 17 appearance on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.
Uche died in 2025 from brain cancer. He and Sheinelle share son Kayin, 16, and 13-year-old twins Uche and Clara.
Woods’ words rang true to Sheinelle.

“I’m sitting here and watching her talk, and whenever you meet someone who has lost a parent and they are your age,” Sheinelle said, gesturing to Woods, “as a mom I’m watching, I’m like, ‘OK, they’re going to be OK.’
“‘They can still have new wins. They can still find love. They can be beautiful. It’s going to be OK.’ And, so, you do that just by being yourself,” she added.

Woods is the founder and CEO of fashion line Woods by Jordyn and is engaged to New York Knicks star Karl-Anthony Towns. Her father died in January 2017 after being diagnosed with cancer. She was 19 years old.
Woods said she continued to see signs her father was with her, even after his death.

“For me, when my dad passed, it was one of the darkest times of my life. But then things started happening where I’m like, ‘Only you could make this happen,’” she said. “You gain your most powerful guardian angel, and even though you’re not here in the physical, I know you’re pulling strings.”
Sheinelle has opened up about how she navigates mourning Uche’s death while also charting a course for her and her kids to move forward.

“As a mom, it’s tough because you don’t want to make them sad,” she said on the CNN podcast “All There Is With Anderson Cooper.” “I have three teenagers. So, there’s a tap dance as their mom, allowing us all to be vulnerable. And there’s a time and a space for us to reflect, and also a time for them to show we can’t move on, we can move forward with that.”
Sheinelle had previously called Uche’s death “a beautiful nightmare” in an interview with Savannah Guthrie in September 2025.
“We don’t move on, but we move forward with our loved ones,” she said. “I hope that just by me being on the set and me returning to work, it’s like, ‘OK, if I can do it, so can you, right?’

“So don’t get me wrong, my heart still hurts,” she continued. “So if you see me now and you see me laughing, or you turn on the morning show and I’m laughing or having a good time, you root for me ... because I’m fighting for my joy.”

title: ‘Love Island USA’ Power Rankings: 2 Superfans Recap This Week’s Winners, Losers and Biggest Surpises
summary: TODAY.com’s “Love Island USA” Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin share their thoughts on the “You’ve Got Mail” recoupling and the next America’s vote.
story: In TODAY.com’s new weekly “Love Island USA” column, content creators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr Griffin break down the latest group of Season 8 episodes, sharing their thoughts on the new bombshells, couplings and dumping. Check back here over the next six weeks to read the duo’s hot takes and follow @tessdhiggins and @sallydarrgriffin on their socials to keep up with them throughout the week.

In the latest episode of “Love Island USA” Season 8, every islander poured their hearts out in handwritten letters. But while some had their feelings reciprocated, others were essentially left on read.
This week, the show switched up its usual firepit recouplings and instead had each contestant pen a sweet letter expressing who they wanted to couple up with.

Most of the islanders sent letters to each other. But two had empty mailboxes, leaving them single. At the end of the recoupling twist, host Ariana Madix announced that the single islanders would pair up, creating a new couple.
Here are the current couples as of Episode 13…
Trinity and Bryce
Trinity and Bryce
Melanie and Sincere
Melanie and Sincere
Kenzie and Corbin
Kenzie and Corbin
Sol and Caleb
Sol and Caleb
Aniya and KC
Aniya and KC
Kayda and Zach
Kayda and Zach
Jen and Gabriel
Jen and Gabriel

However, those new pairs will soon face a bigger test as the show has announced that Casa Amor is right around the corner. Before the drama ramps up even more, TODAY.com’s Season 8 commentators Tess Higgins and Sally Darr are back to discuss the villa shakeup.
Sally Darr enjoyed seeing the love triangle between Sincere, Melanie and Sol come to an end … at least for now.

“I honestly thought (Sincere) was going to pick Sol, but he picked Melanie. I’m hoping that he’s finally done being messy and going back and forth between these two girls about who his number one is,” she shares. “But honestly, if he keeps talking to Sol, I wouldn’t be shocked.”
Tess’ “hot take” is that she’s back on the Melanie and Sincere ship after previously naming them as the “best couple” in TODAY.com’s first power rankings of the season.

“I think it’s interesting to see them come back to each other, like with a lot of enthusiasm, after everything that went down,” Tess explains. “Their connection does seem to feel very real and very dynamic, so I’m interested to keep watching them and hopefully have my opinion change more favorably towards them, too.”
Below the two share more thoughts about the most recent episode. Plus, they predict the results of the newest fan vote, which asks viewers to select their favorite couple.
Read on to hear the duo’s thoughts about the status of the villa as Casa Amor looms in the distance.
Best Moment

Tess: “Bryce and Trinity reading their letters to each other during the recoupling. Of course, they went first because they were the most obvious couple to get paired together. But I felt like they really expressed themselves well. You know, the music behind them was really emotional and sweet. I think it created a really positive vibe to start off that whole recoupling, and I just really enjoy them together.”

Sally Darr: “The best moment was finally seeing Sincere’s decision. We’ve been waiting for this for a while. I honestly thought he was going to pick Sol, but he picked Melanie. I’m hoping that he’s finally done being messy and going back and forth between these two girls about who his number one is. But honestly, if he keeps talking to Sol, I wouldn’t be shocked.”
Biggest Surprise

Tess: “We got another recoupling. I feel like we just had the ‘America votes’ recoupling on Friday, so there’s only been a few episodes for things to further develop. And everything just reverted back to exactly the way things were before. So I was just kind of shocked that they did that so soon and didn’t let these couples marinate a little bit longer.”
Sally Darr: “I think the biggest surprise was Sol and Caleb staying. I honestly thought they were going to get dumped, but Ariana put them together, and now they’re a couple.”
Best Couple

Tess: “Still Trinity and Bryce. I think they are just so funny. They are really fun to watch, and just upbeat and positive. Maybe this is a hot take. My second choice would be Melanie and Sincere, just because I think it’s interesting to see them come back to each other, like with a lot of enthusiasm, after everything that went down. Their connection does seem to feel very real and very dynamic, so I’m interested to keep watching them and hopefully have my opinion change more favorably towards them, too.”

Sally Darr: “I think Aniya and KC are the best couple right now. They seem really sweet. I’m happy that they’re finally on the same page, and I hope that they stay in their couple and continue to grow.”
Biggest Winner

Tess: “Melanie, because I think the audience has really done a 180 on her in the past week or so. I think a lot of people were frustrated with the way that she went about things with Sincere and kind of how heated she was getting, like she was on the verge of a crash out. But she’s really softened over the past week. We’ve seen her more vulnerable side come out. She’s been very open with everyone, and seeing her pair back up with Sincere, and them both being enthusiastic about it tonight, I think may be turning the tides a little bit for both of them.”

Sally Darr: “Melanie is probably the biggest winner tonight. She was picked by Sincere. I wouldn’t call it a win necessarily, because he’s been messy. And if I were Melanie, I probably would have been done with his antics a while ago. But there was a battle for Sincere. Melanie came out the winner, and Sol came out the loser. I would also say Caleb and Sol are big winners tonight, because I think they thought they were going to get dumped. You could see it on their faces, they were shocked.”
At Risk of Being Dumped

Tess: “Caleb and Sol, because they were just forced together as a result of the recoupling. Like, these are two people who did not want to be together in the first place. It just feels silly to have them in the audience vote. Because who’s going to vote for them? We all know that they weren’t actually intended to be a couple, and there was nothing really going on there. So I feel like they’re kind of just going to get dumped by default.”

Sally Darr: “Tonight, the public votes for their favorite couple. I think the couple in last place will be Jen and Gabriel. I don’t think there’s super strong support for them, not because they’re not liked, but because they’re not OGs. People might not feel like they know them super well. Caleb and Sol are an obvious weak couple, but I think they’ll actually have a lot of people voting for them, myself included, because people think they were done a little bit dirty by Kenzie and Sincere. Of course, we don’t know how this public vote is going to come into play. Is the couple in last place just going to get dumped? Are they going to do the bottom three couples and then make the islanders that are still safe in the villa vote between those three bottom couples? We’re not sure. But I think Jen and Gabriel will be in last place for the public vote, so we’ll see how it plays out.”

title: Read With Jenna Jr. 2026: See All 21 Books on This Year’s Kid-Friendly List
summary: Read With Jenna Jr. 2026: Find all the books on the 2026 summer reading list.
story: Summer is here, which means one thing: Summer reading! The kids in your life might have school-assigned books, but this list will make reading for fun that much more feasible.
Jenna Bush Hager has rounded up a list of books for all age groups that hopefully will either feed into, or spark, a love of reading.
For Jenna, reading is something that bonds her with her three children, Mila, 13, Poppy, 10, and Hal, 6.
“We read every night together as a family,” Jenna once told TODAY. “Teaching a kid or watching a kid learn how to read is evolutionarily one of the most beautiful things that can happen as a parent.”
Read past editions of Read With Jenna Jr.
2025 Read With Jenna Jr. list2024 Read With Jenna Jr. list2023 Read With Jenna Jr. list
2025 Read With Jenna Jr. list
2024 Read With Jenna Jr. list
2023 Read With Jenna Jr. list
Below, find all 21 books on this year’s list.
Picture Books (Ages 4-8)
"Better Pets" by Leigh Bardugo
A hilarious picture book celebrating perfect pets everywhere.

Best friends Yvette and Evan live in a world where everyone has a pet — except for them. The Better Pets emporium is here, and the choices are endless. The witty and playful “Better Pets” is the newest picture book for #1 New York Times bestselling author Leigh Bardugo and international bestselling illustrator Liz Climo.
"Otherwise Known as Judy the Great: A Poetic Ode to Judy Blume" by Selina Alko
An inspiring collection of poems celebrating the childhood of iconic author Judy Blume.

The first and only authorized picture book biography of Judy Blume takes shape in this poetic new title. “Otherwise Known as Judy the Great” from award-winning author Selina Alko transforms personal insight from Blume herself into a poetry collection surrounding Blume’s childhood, from fear surrounding growing up Jewish in a post-World War II world to learning to use storytelling as a way to control conflict. This “poetic ode to Judy Blume” recounts the building blocks of this intergenerationally influential American writer.
"Tiny T. Rex Tries Soccer" by Jonathan Stutzman
Tiny T. Rex learns how to play his favorite sport with his best friends.

The New York Times best-selling Tiny T. Rex series from Jonathan Stutzman is back — this time, to teach kids (or readers of any age) that trying new things can be a blast. First up: soccer, just in time for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Tiny T. Rex gets into shape and ready to play with new teammates and friends in this uplifting and energetic picture book (with stickers inside!).
"We Are Mighty 12 Ordinary Americans Who Did the Next Needed Thing" by Sharon McMahon
A journey through twelve everyday Americans turned influential changemakers through kindness and bravery.

Happy 250th birthday, America! Just in time to celebrate, this debut children’s book by author and public speaker Sharon McMahon shares 12 pioneers who may not be in children’s textbooks — but should be. From lighthouse keeper Virginia Randolph to suffragist and activist Wa-Tho-Huk, this illustrated collection is a diverse and real depiction of the minds behind the scenes of America’s progress.
"Axl the Axolotl Is Not a Frog" by Benj Pasek, Justin Paul and John Paul Brammer
The songwriting duo behind "The Greatest Showman" brings a comedic and heartfelt story of belonging.

Rather than trying to change who he is, this axolotl is setting the record straight on his identity: In between the groups of fish, lizards and frogs, Axl doesn’t quite fit into any of the set categories. This picture book from EGOT-winning songwriters is about embracing what makes you different and educating kids on the amazing world of axolotls.
"Loops" by Jashar Awan
A comedic confidence-booster about wearing your first pair of big-kid shoes with laces.

This comedic picture book lets children know that learning to tie “big kid” shoes is a process that can take determination and self-confidence — and, from caregivers, support and patience. “Loops” is here to show how practice can turn into perfect.
"Fireworks" by Matthew Burgess
Winner of the 2026 Caldecott Medal is a July 4th adventure of two siblings anticipating fireworks.

This dazzling New York Public Library Best Illustrated Children’s Book of the Year is just in time for this summer season. Depicted through beautiful illustrations, two city siblings take in the festive bright spectacle as a break from the normally fluorescent city lights.
Middle Grade (Ages 8-12)
"A Fishboy Named . . . Sashimi" by Dan Santat
Chaos ensues when a fish boy out of water sneaks into the human world.

“A Fishboy Named Sashimi” is about Sashimi, a fishboy who leaves the ocean and disguises himself as a student at an elementary school while searching for others like him. When the town begins hunting a mysterious “Beast of Barnacle Bay” that sounds suspiciously like Sashimi, he must rely on new friends, courage and plenty of laughs to find where he truly belongs.
"The Unlikely Tale of Chase and Finnegan" by Jasmine Warga
A cheetah cub and rescue dog become an unlikely duo in this story about friendship and coming-of-age.

“The Unlikely Tale of Chase and Finnegan” by Jasmine Warga tells the heartwarming story of Chase, an orphaned cheetah cub, and Finnegan, a rescue dog, who form an unexpected friendship at a zoo. Together, they help each other gain confidence and discover a sense of family in a moving tale about friendship, belonging and the power of connection.
"The Endless Game" by J.D. Amato
A multi-generational game of capture the flag teaches the kids of Lakeside, Illinois about more than winning.

“The Endless Game” by J.D. Amato follows new kid Fred as he gets pulled into an intense, decades-long game of Capture the Flag. As he settles into his new team, Fred learns the rules and history of the game while finding his place in a tight-knit group in this action-packed, funny and adventurous story about friendship and childhood summers.
"Poetry Comics" by Grant Snider

“Poetry Comics” by Grant Snider is a collection of short comics and poems that explore creativity, nature, books and everyday life. This book is perfect for young readers of comics and graphic novels, aspiring poets, writers and cartoonists, as well as parents and educators who want a fun and engaging way to introduce kids to poetry.
"Troubling Tonsils!" by Aaron Reynolds
Things get creepy and silly in the world of "Jasper Rabbit" when Charlie Marmot loses his tonsils, literally.

“Troubling Tonsils” by Aaron Reynolds and Peter Brown is a silly story about a boy who believes his tonsils have a mind of their own and are causing chaos. When he decides to keep them in a jar for show-and-tell, strange slurping sounds and a surprise doctor visit make him wonder if the tonsils might be getting revenge.
"Wombat Waiting" by Katherine Applegate
A stray dog and young boy pair up on a journey of healing following a Californian wildfire.

“Wombat Waiting” by Katherine Applegate follows a homeless dog nicknamed “Wombat” after a devastating fire, who settles near a makeshift community center and refuses to leave her bench. Believing she is a “destiny dog” guided by an inner “Voice,” she waits for the person she feels is meant to find her, even as the chances of that reunion remain uncertain.
"The Moon Without Stars" by Chanel Miller
Luna's life is changed when one of her zines takes off, as she navigates the difficulties of 7th grade.

“The Moon Without Stars” by Chanel Miller centers on Luna, a quiet seventh grader who loves writing and making zines with her best friend, until unexpected popularity pulls her into a new social circle that challenges who she is. The book is a funny, heartfelt story about identity, friendship and figuring out how to stay true to yourself during the ups and downs of middle school.
Young Adult (Ages 13 and Up)
"Soundtrack" by Jason Reynolds
A raw, resonant journey about friendship and finding your voice set in N.Y.C.'s creative scene.

The award-winning full-cast audiobook of “Soundtrack” is officially on the page. This story follows the fictional all-black punk band The Bed-Stuy Magic Dusters as they navigate the emerging music scene of New York City in the early 2000s. Things aren’t always upbeat for these bandmates, as they face personal hardships and the struggles of making it in one of the toughest industries and cities.
"Love Me Tomorrow" by Emiko Jean
A high-school romance about the power of love through a race to uncover the writer of an anonymous letter.

It’s hard not to believe in love when the love of your life starts sending you love letters from years in the future. This is what happens to 17-year-old Emma Nakamura-Thatcher, and this novel follows her pursuit to discover who is traveling through time to profess their love.
"The Wildest Dreams Bookshop" by Gracie Page
A cozy, seaside romantic comedy about hopeful romance after heartbreak.

When Anna heads to her aunt’s seaside bookshop to escape hometown drama and work for the summer, she finds something better than her next summer read: an intriguing surfer boy. This sweet opposites-attract romantic comedy novel is the first installment of Page’s bookshop series.
"The Gravewood" by Kelly Andrew
A new vampire romance exploring obsession and loyalty in a journey through a dark forest.

This first part to the new dark and complex vampire romance duology explores what happens when the boundaries of an impassable forest, the Gravewood, are tested. When Shea Parker’s best friend vanishes into this forbidden forest, she seeks out the help of a vampiric, forest-ruling gang and their leader, Oliver Lysander. Essentially the same as making a deal with the devil, Shea has a lot at stake to get her friend back. First thing: blood.
"Heir" by Sabaa Tahir
A fantastical adventure between an unlikely trio fueled by vengeance, love and power.

This continuation of her “An Ember in the Ashes” series brings characters from a new generation with their own set of mysteries to solve and power, greed and heartbreak to navigate in the Empire. This romance fantasy has received praise from TIME Magazine, E!News!, the New York Times and Good Morning America.
"The Gilded Blade" by Jennifer Lynn Barnes
The finale to the series of high-risk games to win money, love, power and revenge.

The final installment of the Inheritance Games Saga, this international adventure features the ultimate fight to survive a game of intermingled mysteries. From wealth to romance to thrill, this detective story has it all.
"Thornbird" by E. Kennedy
A crime thriller by the Off Campus series's bestselling author filled with twists and dark family secrets.

Still riding the “Off Campus” momentum, Elle Kennedy is here with yet another highly praised story. This time, she takes on crime, as the novel follows an infamous serial killer’s daughter and her journey uncovering secrets darker than most families find in the archives.

title: Jelly Roll Files For Divorce From Bunnie XO 2 Months Before 10-Year Wedding Anniversary
summary: Jelly Roll has filed for divorce from his wife, Bunnie XO. What to know.
story: Jelly Roll has filed for divorce from his wife, Bunnie XO, according to court records from Williamson County, Tennessee.
NBC News has reached out to lawyers for the singer and Bunnie XO but have yet to hear back.

The “Need a Favor” singer, whose real name is Jason DeFord, filed for divorce on May 18 from the “Dumb Blonde Podcast” host, whose real name is Alisa DeFord. They separated May 9, with Jelly Roll citing irreconcilable differences as the reason for the breakup.

The couple met in 2015 and married in August 2016. Jelly Roll has two children from previous relationships, Bailee Ann, 18, and Noah Buddy, 9. Bunnie XO has often posted about being a proud stepmother to her husband’s kids.
Bailee Ann addressed the divorce on her TikTok story June 16.
“I am disgusted at how invested everyone is in a very clearly private family matter,” she wrote.
In a follow-up post June 17, she also thanked followers for showing her “love and kindness through everything.”

“At the end of the day, no matter what, we are all humans with feelings and that is worthy of compassion,” she added. “Thank you for the kind messages, the words of support, and those of you who go out of your way to make me feel protected and seen. I love yall.”

In Bunnie XO’s February 2026 memoir, “Stripped Down: Unfiltered and Unapologetic,” she provided an update on her and Jelly Roll’s efforts to welcome a child together, writing they had found a surrogate and hoped to have twins, per People.
In June 2024, Bunnie XO and Jelly Roll got candid about trying IVF in the hopes of having a child.

“We genuinely never thought we’d want to add to our family but something changed this year & we both just want a piece of us together to add to our already perfect family w/Bailee and Noah,” Bunnie XO said on Instagram at the time.
In 2025, Jelly Roll revealed he had previously cheated on his wife.
“I don’t talk about this publicly at all but one of the worst moments of my adulthood was when I had an affair on my wife,” he said on the “Human School” podcast.
“Like, I know I’m in love with this woman,” he said. “It just really, really, really blew me back. And I did a lot of work to repair that relationship.”
He also said they were then “stronger than we could have ever been.”
“I’m proud of who we are today,” he added at the time. ”I truly am.”
Jelly Roll’s past infidelity prompted some backlash on social media. One person blasted Bunnie XO on Instagram for staying with him, and she defended her decision in a comment back.

“It actually takes a stronger woman to face pain head-on, do the work, and rebuild with the man she loves — instead of running or gossiping,” she wrote in response. “Growth isn’t weakness, it’s grace. But not everyone’s built for that kind of strength. I pray you never have to fell that pain bc you’re judging another woman’s life.”
In 2024, Bunnie XO revealed she and Jelly Roll had separated in 2018.

“Who knew that us breaking up in 2018, me moving back to Vegas & you coming to get me back — would have put us on this wild journey called life,” she wrote in a TikTok video. “We finally committed to each other & did everything we promised each other the first night in 2016. Our castle in the sand had to crumble so we could rebuild on solid ground I yuh you so mushhh.”

title: First Look at Keanu Reeves and Gard Hollinger’s Docuseries ‘Hooligans: The Arch Racing Project’
summary: Keanu Reeves and Gard Hollinger team up for a racing docuseries, “Hooligans: The Arch Racing Project,” and TODAY.com has the first trailer.
story: “To be or not to be racing?” That is the question that Keanu Reeves and Gard Hollinger asked themselves as they stepped into the high stakes world of professional motorcycle racing.

The friends and founders of Arch Motorcycle shifted from building motorcycles to racing in MotoAmerica, a professional racing league. They documented their journey in “Hooligans: The Arch Racing Project with Keanu Reeves & Gard Hollinger.” And now, TODAY.com has your exclusive first look at the trailer.

The Samsung TV Plus six-part docuseries takes viewers along for the ride as the Arch team competes in the Super Hooligan National Championship. Together, the duo look to prove they have what it takes to go up against the big shots.

“From the garage to the grid, ‘Hooligans: The Arch Racing Project’ captures the ambition and adrenaline behind an underdog racing story fueled by passion, performance and the pursuit of excellence,” the press release reads.
Founded in 2011, Reeves previously shared that he decided to create Arch Motorcycles Company after looking into customizing a Harley-Davidson and being introduced to Hollinger.

“Over the course of four, five years, he designed and built a motorcycle. That motorcycle was amazing, he’s amazing. And I said to him, ‘That needs to be in the world. Do you want to start a motorcycle company?’” the actor told Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist about their partnership in 2019.
At the time, he said they had been selling motorcycles for about four years.
Reeves also shared that he started riding bikes at 22 years old, “and Gard started when he was, like, 8.”
“Riding a motorcycle is fantastic. You’re in the element,” he enthusiastically said about the experience. “You can think. You can get away. You can focus. You can feel. You can just let it go.”
“Hooligans: The Arch Racing Project” premieres Sunday, July 12, at 9 p.m. ET on Samsung TV Network (STN). New episodes premiere every Sunday through August 16.

title: Craig Melvin Reveals Deep-Seated Hatred for Peanut Butter: ‘They Perverted the Peanut!’
summary: TODAY anchor Craig Melvin went on a hilarious rant about peanut butter to Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers, saying “they perverted the peanut.”
story: Comedians Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers are known to inspire others to join in on their “I Don’t Think So, Honey!” rants. For Jenna Bush Hager, her on-brand hot topic of choice was book banning.
And when the comedians stopped by TODAY, Craig Melvin chose to speak his truth about his latest food-related annoyance.

Yang and Rogers, who co-host the hit pop culture podcast “Las Culturistas,” spoke with Craig ahead of the 5th annual Las Culturistas Culture Awards premiere to discuss their journey from a makeshift recording studio to podcast fame, Yang’s recent departure from “Saturday Night Live” and the excitement surrounding the televised success of their distinct award show.

“I Don’t Think So, Honey!” — also known as “IDTSH” — is a feature comedy rant segment of “Las Culturistas.” IDTSH entails an uninterrupted (exactly one minute, to be exact) rant session about a disliked trend, pop culture moment or pet peeve.
Rogers and Yang encouraged Craig to get a minute of his own to let out any hot topics on his mind.

Despite being hesitant about his ability to go off the fly with even a 30-second version of IDTSH, Craig went right into it with some salty history, and from there, he barely took a breath. His victim? Peanuts, and anyone who loves peanut butter.

“George Washington Carver created the peanut ... he created 350 other products, none of them peanut butter,” Craig explained. “At some point, someone decided they wanted to make a buck. They perverted the peanut!”

After going after the “cement-y” texture and “terrible” smell, Craig made a very true point about the peanut’s imposter status in the world of nuts: “Moreover, not even a nut, it’s a legume! It’s an edible seed inside a pod. It’s not even a real nut!”
That’s right: A peanut allergy is not a nut allergy — it’s a legume allergy!
Craig didn’t stop there. He saluted — literally — the nuts that have a very real and deserving place in the nut world.
“Walnuts? Salute. Pistachio? Salute. Cashew? Salute. Peanut butter? I don’t think so, honey!” Craig finished with a titular, mic-drop-worthy last statement to round out his IDTSH time.
Everyone in the studio gestured in support of Craig’s peanut passion — or, rather anti-peanut passion.
“That was an all-timer, sir,” Rogers said as he shook Craig’s hand.

Although this rendition of IDTSH with Craig was exclusive to TODAY, the star-studded comedy award show is coming to screens. The Las Culturistas Culture Awards were pre-taped on May 30 at The United Theater on Broadway in Los Angeles, California, and the full show will air on Bravo and Peacock June 17 at 9 p.m. ET/PT.
(Bravo and Peacock are part of our parent company, NBCUniversal.)

title: Jelly Roll’s Daughter, 18, Thanks Followers for ‘Empathy’ Amid His Split from Bunnie XO
summary: Jelly Roll’s daughter Bailee Ann DeFord spoke out about the reaction to her father filing for divorce from her stepmother, Bunnie XO.
story: Jelly Roll’s daughter, 18-year-old Bailee Ann DeFord, said her father’s split from wife Bunnie XO is a “private family matter,” calling the public interest surrounding it “crazy.”
Bailee Ann posted a photo and message to her TikTok story expressing “disgust” at the attention generated by her father filing for divorce from her stepmother.
“Oh & one more thing I am disgusted at how invested everyone is in a very clearly private family matter,” she wrote over a picture of herself. “It’s fkn crazy.
“Go on somewhere yall. Worry bout your house- not mine. I’m not speaking on it — yet.”
Bailee Ann is Jelly Roll’s daughter with Felicia Beckwith. He is also father to son Noah Buddy with Melisa Ann Cowell.

Jelly Roll was granted primary custody of Bailee Ann when she was 8, he revealed on a 2025 episode of “The Pivot” podcast. He credited wife Bunnie XO with prioritizing being there for Bailee Ann, and over the years, the 18-year-old has often spoken about her close relationship with her stepmom.
In a follow-up post June 17, Bailee Ann thanked followers for their support.
“On a more positive note — thank you to everyone who is showing me so much love and kindness through everything,” she wrote.

“At the end of the day, no matter what, we are all humans with feelings and that is worthy of compassion. Thank you for the kind messages, the words of support, and those of you who go out of your way to make me feel protected and seen. I love yall.

“I will never forget those of you that have shown me empathy when I’m seeing a lot of hate. Thank you. So much. And to everyone else, I hope humanity can evolve to a place where kindness comes first. I understand & respect opinions and that a public profile is a public profile, but that doesn’t mean we throw away our human decency. I love ALL of you,” she concluded.
Jelly Roll, who married Bunnie XO in 2016, filed for divorce in Tennessee on May 18.

The couple do not have kids, but the “Dumb Blonde Podcast” host wrote in her 2026 memoir, “Stripped Down: Unfiltered and Unapologetic,” that they hoped to have twins and had found a surrogate, according to People.
Bunnie XO first revealed that they wanted to expand their family in a June 2024 post on Instagram.

“We genuinely never thought we’d want to add to our family but something changed this year & we both just want a piece of us together to add to our already perfect family w/Bailee and Noah,” Bunnie XO said at the time.
Bunnie XO has often shared social media posts expressing her joy of being a stepmother and has appeared in posts with Bailee Ann.
In 2024, she lashed out against people who criticized her stepdaughter and husband for their weights.

“For you to even come on my page and say something about my underage daughter’s body shows the type of human you are and I just hope you have the day that you deserve,” Bunnie XO said on TikTok at the time.

title: Kevin Bacon Is Changing His Name for a Day. Here’s Why
summary: Kevin Bacon is temporarily changing his last name in support of animals. Find out what his new moniker is and why he wouldn’t mind changing his first name, too.
story: Since his meteoric rise to fame in the 1984 movie “Footloose,” fans have known actor Kevin Bacon as, well, Kevin Bacon.
Not any more.
The veteran star of film and television has decided to turn over a new leaf — or legume if you will — by changing his last name from Bacon to Bean.
“I just thought it seemed like such a funny idea,” Bacon tells TODAY.com during a Zoom interview. And that’s exactly what the tongue-in-cheek switcheroo is all about.

Bacon is teaming up with Humane World for Animals (formerly called the Humane Society of the United States) for “Beansday,” a new campaign encouraging people to swap out meat for beans on Wednesdays. “It can have an impact on animal’s lives, on the environment, on your pocketbook,” Bacon says of the push.
In support of the launch, the “Tremors” star is temporarily changing his last name from Bacon to Bean and donning a suit made entirely of the legumes in a new PSA for the campaign.
“I love animals. I thought it was a clever and funny idea; anything that can kind of shake up our everyday approach to food and eating animals is just an interesting kind of twist.”
The cute play on his last name is intended to raise awareness around food choices and encourage people to embrace more plant-based options for the sake of both animals and the planet.
Bacon, who lives on a 40-acre farm with wife, Kyra Sedgwick, knows a little something about animals given he’s raising a bunch of them, including horses, goats, pigs, cows and chickens.
“It’s like that connection that you have to just go and spend a little time with a horse or a goat or a pig. I love the pigs, I’m crazy about them,” says Bacon. “I find it therapeutic and calming.”

Bacon fans are likely familiar with his Instagram posts, in which he can regularly be found singing songs to his four-legged friends, often joined by Sedgwick and their two adult children, Travis, 36, and Sosie, 34.

“When I get out to sing to the goats or the pigs or whatever, a lot of times what you’re seeing is, like, Take 30,” he explains, saying that the animals don’t always cooperate, especially if he’s in the middle of composing a song.
“I’ll have the selfie stick, and the phone, and the lyrics ... and the goats, right at the moment when I think I’m done, will grab the lyrics and eat them because they love paper,” says Bacon.
But that’s to be expected, right?
“There’s a lot of stuff that they don’t eat,” he says. “Someone has told me goats eat everything, but they don’t. Pigs eat everything. But goats, they’re a little more picky.”
Even so, they apparently have an appetite for paper. “Especially if I’ve written something on it that I have to try to remember,” Bacon says laughing.
Along with their penchant for song lyrics, the goats also have a habit of crashing his shoots.

“There’s been plenty of times when I’ve been right towards the end of the song and the goat will just turn around and poop,” says Bacon. “When goats poop, it’s like a rainstorm of pellets. It’s funny, but probably not what people want to see.”
Given his “new” last name and all, Bacon is likely to field some questions about the moniker change. Fortunately, he’s used it.
“Some people have actually asked me if I’ve changed my name to ‘Bacon.’ I think to myself, ‘Well, what kind of lunatic would want to suffer a lifetime of food jokes?’’ he asks.

While changing his last name, for real, hasn’t crossed his mind, his first name is a different story. “I don’t mind the name ‘Bacon,’ but I don’t like the name ‘Kevin.’ I’ve never liked that name,” he says.

“What I find – and this could completely be my imagination – but what I see in movies and television, is that whenever there’s someone annoying or just kind of a s----y personality, or a kid who’s a pain in the a--, they always name him ‘Kevin,’” he says.
“Someone actually told me that in certain parts of the UK, they use it as an insult. Like, ‘He’s a Kevin.’”
Fortunately, Bacon has found a workaround.
“What’s weird about it is that I don’t mind the name ‘Kev.’ To me that’s a nice sounding name,” he says.

“But, of course, then there’s that funny thing where you feel like you’ve been ‘Kev-d’ quickly. Now, I’ve given that up. I’m like, ‘If you want to ‘Kev’ me right from the beginning, I’m totally fine with that.’”

title: What Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO Have Said About Their Marriage Over the Years
summary: After nearly 10 years of marriage, Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO are separating. Read what they have shared about their relationship.
story: After nearly 10 years of marriage, Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO are separating.

The Grammy winner, whose real name is Jason DeFord, filed for divorce on May 18 from the podcast host, whose real name is Alisa DeFord, according to court documents obtained by NBC News from Williamson County, Tennessee.
Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO’s lawyers have not responded to NBC News’ request for a comment.

The two, who have been married since August 2016, have spoken about the ups and downs of their relationship and raising children together. Jelly Roll has an 18-year-old daughter named Bailee Ann and 9-year-old son named Noah Buddy. A proud stepmom, Bunnie XO frequently gushes about the children on social media and in interviews.

Earlier this year, Bunnie XO shared in her memoir “Stripped Down: Unfiltered and Unapologetic” that she and the singer were planning to expand their family. She wrote that they had found a surrogate and were hoping to have twins, People reported in February.

“Now that we’re finally stable financially and somewhat emotionally after all the healing we’ve done over this decade together, we’re talking about the future — ­including growing our family,” she wrote at the time in her book. “J and I have a surrogate, the sweetest woman ever, and soon I’ll be starting my IVF stims.”

Bunnie XO previously opened up about the couple’s IVF journey. She and Jelly Roll have also publicly discussed issues with infidelity and a prior split. Read on to learn what the two have shared about the marriage over the years.
On Meeting at Jelly Roll’s Concert

Music brought Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO together. He confirmed on Instagram in 2022 that he met his future wife after she stopped by the Las Vegas Country Saloon when he was performing one night in 2015.

“I’ll never forget that night on Fremont street that I met you standing by the bar at what was then called the ‘LVCS’ (Las Vegas country saloon) — 7 years later to stand on that same street right after we played one of the biggest shows they’ve had on Fremont Street is a feeling I’ll never forget,” he wrote, in part, beside photos of them linking hands at the bar.
A year later, he asked her to marry him at a Yelawolf and Deftones concert in Las Vegas, he said during a podcast interview. They married the same night he popped the question.
When reflecting on their quick nuptials, Bunnie Xo said “I just think when you know, you know” on her podcast in April 2021. However, she acknowledged that the two struggled early on.
She described the first years of their marriage as “a rodeo.”

“You’re going to hang on because you guys don’t really know each other ... figuring everything out and you hop into the frying pan (with) hot grease, but it’s so worth it in the end,” she shared, adding that the two went to couples therapy.
On Being in an Open Relationship
In an April 2021 episode of her podcast, Bunnie XO said she and Jelly Roll were in an open relationship.
She explained, “We allow each other to be who we want to be. If I want to sleep with another guy, I can go sleep with another guy. Like, that’s not a problem.”
On Their 2018 Breakup
The latest divorce filing is not the first time Bunnie XO and Jelly Roll have called it quits. Bunnie XO shared in a May 2024 TikTok that the two briefly separated two years into their marriage.

In a post celebrating her husband as her “bestie,” the “Dumb Blonde Podcast” host wrote, “Who knew that us breaking up in 2018, me moving back to Vegas & you coming to get me back — would have put us on this wild journey called life.”
She said after the breakup they decided to fully commit to each other and live up to the promises they made in 2016.
“Our castle in the sand had to crumble so we could rebuild on solid ground,” she wrote before expressing her love for her husband.
On Jelly Roll Cheating in the Past
Jelly Roll and Bunnie XO previously hit a rough patch due to infidelity.
He shared on the “Human School” podcast in October 2025 that he cheated on Bunnie XO in the past and put in “a lot of work” to fix their marriage.
“I don’t talk about this publicly at all but one of the worst moments of my adulthood was when I had an affair on my wife,” he revealed.
After working through their problems, he said that they were “stronger than we could have ever been.”
“I’m proud of who we are today. I truly am,” he continued.
At the time, an Instagram user criticized Bunnie XO for staying with Jelly Roll. She responded to the comment and defended her decision.
Bunnie XO replied, “It actually takes a stronger woman to face pain head-on, do the work, and rebuild with the man she loves — instead of running or gossiping.”
She said that she was extending grace to her husband.
“But not everyone’s built for that kind of strength. I pray you never have to feel that pain (because) you’re judging another woman’s life,” she wrote.
On Their Plans to Have More Children

In 2024, Jelly Roll appeared on the “Bussin With The Boys” podcast to discuss his weight loss journey. He revealed that his chats with Bunnie XO about having another child played a role in him wanting to change his lifestyle.

Bunnie XO shared a clip of the podcast appearance on her Instagram. In the video, Jelly Roll said, “My wife and I are talking about having a baby.” He explained that raising another child meant that he would need to live until “at least 60” so he could see them go to college.

In a caption, Bunnie XO wrote, “We had planned on doing this privately, but decided our ivf journey needed to be shared because we’ve always been so open. And w/ all odds stacked against us, it’s already been hard & we have only just begun.”

She added, “J & I are SO excited & scared all at the same time. We genuinely never thought we’d want to add to our family but something changed this year & we both just want a piece of us together to add to our already perfect family with Bailee & Noah.”
In December 2025, Bailee threw the couple a surprise prom because neither had attended the dance in high school.
Bunnie XO posted a video that showed the couple dressed to the nines and walking into a room filled with their loved ones and a sign that read, “A Night Under the Stars.”

title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed after Israeli strikes in Lebanon
summary: Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight
story: Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy warned ships not to approach the waterway, which Iran had committed to reopening under the interim peace deal signed this week. It said in a statement that vessels’ safety would be at risk if they did so.

Iran’s top joint military command said the ⁠closure was the “first step” in response to what were described as ‌breaches of ⁠commitments by the U.S. and Israel, according to Iran’s semiofficial Mehr news agency.

In a social media post Saturday, President Donald Trump threatened to impose U.S tolls in the waterway if a final deal with Iran isn’t reached within 60 days. The current agreement establishes toll-free travel through the strait for 60 days.
Trump did not address Iran’s assertion that the strait is currently closed.

In a statement on X that did not acknowledge the apparent closure, U.S. Central Command said that 55 merchant ships transited the strait on Saturday, “moving large amounts of cargo and more than 17 million barrels of oil to global markets.”
U.S. forces “remain present and vigilant to ensure all aspects of the agreement with Iran are adhered to, obeyed, and in full force and effect,” the statement said.

Iran and the U.S. have both shown frustration with Israel for continuing to strike Lebanon despite the deal signed this week, which specified that fighting must end on all fronts, including Lebanon. Though Israel was not a direct party to that deal, Iran has warned that it would consider Israeli strikes a violation of the terms.

Early Saturday, Israeli strikes killed at least 16 people, including two children, according to Lebanese civil defense and media, one day after the U.S. said Israel and Hezbollah had implemented a fresh ceasefire at President Donald Trump’s request.

Israel hit a series of towns across Lebanon’s south early Saturday, Lebanese news agency NNA reported. An airstrike on the town of Arabsalim reportedly killed three people, the agency reported, and a drone strike on the town of Deir al-Zahrani reportedly killed one person. At least seven people remain trapped under the rubble, it said. Lebanon’s army said a soldier was killed between Kfar Rumman and Nabatieh in southern Lebanon.
A previous wave of strikes on Friday killed 83 people, Lebanon’s Ministry of Health said.

A statement from the Israel Defense Forces said Hezbollah, an Iran-backed militant group, had breached the ceasefire and “launched more than 50 projectiles toward IDF soldiers operating in southern Lebanon” overnight, and that Israel had attacked what it described as Hezbollah targets in response. “The IDF remains committed to the ceasefire agreement,” the statement said.
Hezbollah said it had “adhered to the ceasefire since Friday evening,” accusing Israel of making false claims to justify its attacks in an effort to “sabotage the agreement” between Iran and the U.S.

The continued military bombardment has threatened to derail the fragile U.S. peace talks with Iran. Trump’s special envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner were in Switzerland on Saturday “dealing with some of the technical elements of this negotiation,” Vice President JD Vance told Fox News.
Hours after he spoke to Fox, the vice president also departed for Bürgenstock, Switzerland, to join the ongoing negotiations.

Speaking to reporters before he boarded the plane, Vance said that the situation in Lebanon had, “calmed down,” despite news reports and added, “I think we’re going to hopefully make progress on the nuclear issue, hopefully make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue. Those are the two big things that I think we’re going to be focused on.”
Pakistan’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced “technical-level talks” will take place Sunday, with U.S. and Iranian representatives joined by mediators from Qatar and Pakistan.

Esmail Baghaei, a spokesman for Iran’s Foreign Ministry, told Iranian media the trip was a “mission to demand the fulfillment of the other side’s obligations,” adding that “negotiations for a final agreement” will begin when such obligations are implemented.
Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi will lead the Iranian delegation, he said, according to Iranian state TV.

Officials from Tehran and Washington were originally set to meet in Switzerland on Friday to begin 60 days of negotiations on a “final” deal, but those talks were postponed. The negotiations are meant to resolve some of the thorniest issues in the deal that are yet to be agreed upon, including Iran’s nuclear program.

U.S. spy agencies believe that Israel will likely continue to launch attacks against the militant group in Lebanon, potentially jeopardizing the tentative peace deal, according to a source with knowledge of the intelligence assessments.

Israel’s continued strikes on Lebanon have worsened a widening rift between the Trump administration and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his far-right allies, who have criticized the Iran deal as not in Israel’s interest and agitated for more strikes on Lebanon. Netanyahu is also facing a crucial election in October and will likely need the support of the Israeli far right to stay in power.

“Netanyahu is in a challenging political spot,” Jonathan Panikoff, a former career intelligence officer now at the Atlantic Council think tank, told NBC News in an email. “When combined with the broad view that Iran has emerged strategically stronger, Netanyahu finds himself trapped.”

Netanyahu’s vow to occupy southern Lebanon and Israel’s decision to strike both Iran and Lebanon while an initial deal was being hammered out repeatedly delayed talks, fueling frustration among U.S. officials.
Vance hit out at Israeli officials on Thursday, saying that Israel does not appreciate American support.
U.S.-Iran talks postponed as Vance cancels Switzerland trip

Despite the rising tensions over Lebanon, there had been hope that ships trapped in the Persian Gulf will be able to transit through the Strait of Hormuz since the deal was signed. Industry experts have warned, however, that it could take weeks for the shipping traffic to fully normalize, given that the threat of mines still needs to be cleared.
IDF spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin said Friday that Israeli forces will continue to operate in southern Lebanon and “do whatever is necessary to protect our civilians.”
The White House and Israeli prime minister’s office did not immediately respond to requests for comment on the Iranian declaration.

title: Iran peace talks have been chaotic. But confusion has only grown since the MOU was signed.
summary: WASHINGTON — The effort by the U.S. and Iran to negotiate an end to the war has been defined by dramatic fits and starts and punctuated by missile strikes and naval blockades.
story: WASHINGTON — The effort by the U.S. and Iran to negotiate an end to the war has been defined by dramatic fits and starts and punctuated by missile strikes and naval blockades. Yet since a memorandum of understanding was brokered last weekend, the confusion has only grown.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The White House said the agreement had been signed on Sunday by Vice President JD Vance but then announced there would be another signing — this time with a ceremony — on Friday. Also on Sunday, President Donald Trump said he would “fully authorize” the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz in what he described as a “complete” deal. But an hour later, the president said the critical waterway would open once the deal was signed on Friday.

Trump administration officials offered conflicting messages on Monday about when the text of the memorandum would be released, with some saying within the next 24 to 48 hours and others saying not until Friday.

At one point, Trump said while in France for the Group of Seven summit that he might just read the text of the MOU aloud during a news conference. Instead, U.S. officials read the text on a call with reporters during Trump’s news conference.

Then, Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian suddenly re-signed the agreement, the U.S. leader doing so during a dinner at Versailles with French President Emmanuel Macron looking on. And now it’s unclear whether there will be a signing ceremony, or further talks, at all.

The lack of clarity over the proposed peace deal is only the latest murky point in the conflict since the U.S. and Israel launched attacks on Iran in February. Trump has offered shifting reasons for going to war with Iran and given different answers on what he hoped to get out of it. Both Iran and the U.S. have continued to launch strikes, even as leaders say a temporary ceasefire brokered in early April remains in place. Even the MOU differs from what Trump said he wanted out of a deal with Iran.
